meaning: vt.穿透,渗透,看穿,洞察vi.刺入,看穿,渗透,弥漫
examples: Air cannot penetrate between the soil particles, making the soil oxygen-poor.
Our eyes could not penetrate the darkness.
Early-stage tests have shown that the compounds penetrate artery walls and block the bad enzyme.
Generally, said Mr Baker, attackers used any tactic that got results when seeking to penetrate networks.
